frozen_components: base model (used for expert and naive conditional sampling)
architecture.summary: MixSD is a fine-tuning method that constructs supervision targets dynamically by sampling tokens from two conditionals of the base model: an expert conditional observing injected knowledge in context, and a naive conditional reflecting the model's original prior. At each decoding step, tokens are sampled from expert or naive conditionals via Bernoulli mixing with rate α, then used as targets for standard NLL training. The method operates entirely within the base model's own autoregressive distribution, requiring no external teacher.

biblio.title: MixSD: Mixed Contextual Self-Distillation for Knowledge Injection

claims.dataset: KGFact-Small + held-out benchmarks (AIME-2024, MATH-500, GSM8K, HumanEval, MMLU)
claims.metric: average held-out capability (unweighted average across AIME-2024, MATH-500, GSM8K, HumanEval, MMLU)
claims.statement: On KGFact-Small with Qwen3-1.7B, MixSD (α=0.5) achieves 100% training accuracy while retaining 39.9% average held-out capability, compared to standard SFT which achieves 99% training accuracy but retains only 14.3% held-out capability

claims.dataset: KGFunc + held-out benchmarks (AIME-2024, MATH-500, GSM8K, HumanEval, MMLU)
claims.metric: average held-out capability (unweighted average across AIME-2024, MATH-500, GSM8K, HumanEval, MMLU)
claims.statement: On KGFunc with Qwen3-4B-Instruct, MixSD (α=0.5) achieves 89.1% KGFunc-Test accuracy and 67.8% KGFunc-Unseen accuracy while preserving 79.2% average held-out capability, compared to SFT which achieves 72.6% training accuracy but retains only 16.6% held-out capability

claims.dataset: KGFact-Small, KGFact-Large, KGFunc, SimpleQA (aggregated across datasets and checkpoints)
claims.metric: Pearson correlation coefficient (r²) between Fisher alignment ratio and forgetting
claims.statement: Fisher alignment ratio correlates with forgetting at r² = 0.91, 0.88, and 0.87 for 1.7B, 4B, and 8B models respectively, compared to raw weight displacement magnitude which shows much weaker correlation (r² = 0.21, 0.19, 0.11)

claims.metric: percentage of high-NLL tokens in supervision targets (NLL threshold 8)
claims.statement: MixSD with α=0.5 reduces high-NLL tokens (NLL > 8) in supervision targets from 27–42% (SFT baseline) to 4–8%, while retaining 81–98% of unique high-NLL token types that encode new information

claims.metric: held-out capability retention and editing accuracy
claims.statement: On MQuAKE knowledge editing benchmark, MixSD (α=0.5) with Qwen3-4B achieves comparable editing accuracy to SFT while preserving above 90% of held-out capability, compared to MEMIT which preserves nearly 100% capability but achieves only 53–70% editing accuracy across scales

MixSD produces supervision targets with substantially lower per-token negative log-likelihood under the base model compared to SFT ground-truth targets, shifting the target distribution toward higher-probability regions of the base model's autoregressive distribution

claims.dataset: MMLU and other held-out benchmarks after fine-tuning on KGFact-Large
claims.statement: SFT induces qualitatively different failure modes on held-out benchmarks compared to the base model, with pathological leakage (generation of fictional entities from training corpus) and template collapse accounting for majority of errors, whereas MixSD preserves the base model's original error distribution dominated by genuine reasoning errors

MixSD introduces an additional hyperparameter α; while α=0.5 performs well in experiments, the optimal value may differ for other tasks, and the method has not been evaluated on substantially larger models (70B and beyond)
